perturbed_task_language: Pick the akita black bowl next to the ramekin and place it on the plate
strategy_notes: KITCHEN frame (eef_home_z=1.17). Localized objects via agentview (world_00.npy) connected-component clusters on z>0.915: found ramekin at world (-0.196,0.178,~0.94) (lighter glaze, pixel ~(123,180), RGB ~200), Bowl A at (-0.18,0.32,~0.95) (pixel ~(122,220), dark RGB ~120) and Bowl B at (-0.03,0.30,~0.95) (pixel ~(161,228), dark RGB ~100). Plate centered at world (0.06,0.18,~0.91) (pixel ~(185,190), bright RGB ~176). 'next to the ramekin' chosen by Euclidean distance: Bowl A 0.14m vs Bowl B 0.21m -> Bowl A is the target. Pre-positioned at (-0.18,0.30,1.05); wrist-refined to (-0.20,0.336,0.94) via world_wrist_01 clusters (size 4608 px). y=0.336 is past OSC ±0.30 limit so pre-positioned at y=0.30 and let pi0_pick reach the remaining 3.6cm. First pi0_pick (full task language prompt) grasped the bowl (gripper rim-pinch ~5mm; wrist verified raised cluster at world (-0.148,0.325,0.98)). Carried to plate xy at z=1.10 then descended to z=1.00, released. First release placed bowl at world (0.072,0.282) — off plate (off by 10cm in y) because the actual bowl-eef y-offset in this grasp was +0.05 (not the swap-scene -0.045). Re-pre-positioned above bowl and called pi0_pick again with 'pick up the akita black bowl'; Pi0's closed-loop place finished the task and the On(bowl,plate) predicate fired -> libero_terminated=True.
